name: Rhino MCP Server
what_it_does: Rhino 3D MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Rhinoceros 3D (Rhino) — the professional NURBS-based 3D modeling software widely used in architecture, industrial design, and engineering. Enables agents to create and modify 3D geometry, run Grasshopper scripts, query model data, and integrate AI assistance into Rhino-based design workflows.
use_cases: Creating 3D geometry and objects in Rhino from design automation agents
Running Grasshopper parametric design scripts from computational design agents
Querying model dimensions and properties from design review agents
Generating architectural forms and structures from architecture agents
Automating repetitive 3D modeling tasks from engineering workflow agents
AI-assisted industrial product design from product design agents
not_for: Teams not using Rhino 3D (use Blender MCP for Blender, Fusion 360 MCP for Fusion)
Non-professional 3D design (Rhino is expensive professional software)
Real-time rendering and animation (use dedicated rendering tools alongside Rhino)
best_when: An architect, industrial designer, or engineer using Rhino needs AI assistance creating and modifying 3D models — NURBS geometry and Grasshopper parametric design with AI guidance.
avoid_when: You use Blender, SolidWorks, or other 3D tools — use those tools' MCPs. Rhino is specifically for NURBS-based professional design.

auth.notes: No authentication — connects to Rhino running locally. Rhino 3D must be open and running. The MCP communicates with Rhino via its RhinoScript/RhinoCommon API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Rhino 3D requires license: $995 (perpetual) or educational pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Rhino 8: $995 single-user license; Rhino subscription available
pricing.notes: Rhino 3D is commercial software requiring purchase. 90-day evaluation available. MCP server is free open source. Students/educators get significant discounts.

performance.rate_limits: Local operation — no rate limits; complex geometric operations may take time

known_agent_gotchas: Rhino must be open and licensed — not headless deployable
NURBS geometry concepts (curves, surfaces, solids) require domain knowledge for effective agent use
Grasshopper parametric operations may be complex to express via MCP
Rhino API commands differ from general 3D modeling concepts — review RhinoScript documentation
Community MCP targeting niche Rhino user base — limited documentation
Rhino version differences (7 vs 8) may affect API compatibility
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Rhino connection. No network. No credentials. Design files are local. Rhino license required. No security concerns beyond local file access.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local only. No credentials. Design file access is local. Rhino license required.
